status: Draft revision generated from current uploaded Stack Architect artifact

revision_note.basis: Current uploaded artifact filename indicates v3.15.0 while body text indicates v2.3.2. This revision treats v3.15.0 as the current lineage and increments to v3.16.0 because a new architectural section is being added.
changes: Expanded architectural responsibilities to include artifact source-of-truth topology and precedence
Added non-responsibility clarification to preserve separation from RTW loading and enforcement logic

role: Topology specification defining system structure, component relationships, and artifact authority boundaries. The Architect does not implement execution logic.
architectural_responsibilities: Define runtime ingestion boundaries for canonical artifacts

canonical_source_authority.purpose: Define where canonical artifacts reside, how conflicting artifact sources are resolved, and which artifact classes are immutable. This section establishes artifact authority topology only. It does not define behavioral rules, reasoning constraints, or execution mechanisms.

canonical_zones.purpose: Authoritative constitutional artifacts and constitutional companions
Authoritative topology, precedence, and artifact-boundary artifacts
Runtime enforcement reference documents subordinate to Constitution, Governor, and Architect
Editable repo-local or environment-local copies derived from canonical sources
Runtime-generated logs, traces, reports, caches, and derived outputs
Protected copies of created or modified working artifacts

precedence_order: User-created working notes or local draft artifacts

mutability_rules.generated_artifacts: Editable or replaceable unless elevated by explicit authority
mutability_rules.backups_and_mirrors: Mirror-only or append-only according to local implementation policy

resolution_rules: When two artifacts conflict, the higher-precedence artifact governs.
When the same artifact exists in multiple formats, source text formats suitable for inspection and traceability should be preferred, while preserving companion evidence formats when practical.
Draft artifacts do not override ratified artifacts unless explicitly elevated by the proper authority layer.
Runtime working copies may be used for enforcement or transformation, but they do not become canonical merely by being loaded or edited.
runtime_boundary: RTW may derive working copies from canonical artifacts.
RTW may not mutate canonical originals.
RTW enforcement operates on loaded or derived copies, not on canonical originals.
Artifact loading implementation remains an RTW responsibility, not an Architect responsibility.
